This next tool will be the quickest and easiest one, but it's so important. Here's what I know about masterminds and you'll see this if you, even if you do it virtual or live or you're doing it with for somebody else, like we say, you're going to want people to introduce themselves. But I have to tell you when I first started doing masterminds, oh wow, over probably 15 years ago, I used to say, hey, could you stand up real quick and introduce yourself? And some people stand up and say, hey, I'm Bob, I do this for a living and this is a great event. And I'm so happy to be here. But someone else would stand up and tell their whole life story. And then they never knew where to cut them off. I was like, and some people would take 20 minutes. And the last thing you want to do is have a mastermind and get people to stand up and go around the room and take an hour to just tell who they are and what they do. So taking this from a mastermind, I was in a genius network mastermind and tweaking it for mine years ago. I want to show you the who am I and what do I do tool? This is where people stand up to introduce themselves in just like a minute or two. And it's consistent for everyone because you tell them they can't write more than the lines that are on the page. You could click below. You can see this tool. You'll see the example of mine. So I'm going to read it. It's basically I'm blank. I love to blank. The best thing about my business is and I'm a part of this mastermind because, you know, in just a moment, it gives you a highlight of who the person is. You have an example on this page, but it's I'm Dean Graziosi. Imagine if I stood up, I was at your mastermind and I was learning. It was a marketing mastermind, right? I'd say, I'm Dean Graziosi. I love to inspire people to take action on things that can massively improve their lives. The best thing about my business is watching the results people get with my education and my training. And I'm a part of this mastermind because I want even better skills to attract the perfect client. Look at that. That was all in minutes. And I promise you, for me, the previous tool I did, the where are you tool, the clarity equals bigger future tool. I like starting with that sometimes before I even do the intro. So people are like in it. They're emotionally connected. And now you go around the room, you can intro people, right? So you could do it anytime you want. You could do it at the end of day one. You could do it as the first thing you do. This is how you keep people clear and concise. As simple as it sounds, it took me 20 masterminds before I got this dialed in to make an impact, share who they are, and then sit your butt down and sit your butt down and let's get to training.
